signature: def get_falloff_value(distance_normalized: float, falloff: str = "smooth") -> float:
aaa_equivalent: ZBrush/Mudbox brush falloff curve (customizable curve editor)
strength: Provides four named falloff curves including a proper cosine smooth ramp with clamping and unknown-name guard.
weakness: Hard-coded set of 4 curves with no user-definable curve editor, no root/inverse-sharp/middle/gaussian options, and the clamp to 1.5 silently allows out-of-range input where AAA tools expose an editable 2D spline.
upgrade: Replace with a sampled 1D curve LUT driven by a user-editable spline (bezier/catmull) plus common presets (gauss, sharper, root, constant, custom), matching Blender/ZBrush brush curve UX.

aaa_equivalent: UE Landscape brush footprint / Houdini heightfield mask by radius
strength: Correctly uses squared-distance culling in XY before taking the sqrt, returning a sparse weight list.
weakness: O(N) linear scan over every vertex with no spatial acceleration (KD-tree/grid/BVH), XY-only so projects wrong on sloped terrain, no pressure/tilt/azimuth, no anisotropic (elliptical) brush, no alpha-texture mask, and no stroke dab spacing - AAA tools use screen-space projection along the view ray with a spatial index.
upgrade: Add KD-tree/spatial grid accel, ray-project the brush onto the surface (not XY plane), support pressure curve + elliptical shape + alpha-image mask + stroke spacing, and compute weight over geodesic distance for steep slopes.

aaa_equivalent: ZBrush Standard/Inflate brush, UE Landscape Sculpt (raise)
strength: Simple deterministic weighted displacement, easy to test.
weakness: Displaces purely along world Z with no surface-normal option, no accumulation curve / per-dab falloff, no max-height clamp, no layer/undo stack, no pressure-sensitive strength, and no noise/jitter - an AAA raise brush offsets along averaged normal with stroke accumulation and clamps.
upgrade: Offset along smoothed vertex normal (toggle normal vs Z), add per-stroke accumulation/clamp, pressure curve, optional noise modulation, and feed into a layered height buffer for non-destructive sculpting.

strength: Symmetric to raise, tiny and correct for its trivial spec.
weakness: Just a sign-flip of raise - no normal-aligned direction, no floor/minimum clamp, no distinct 'dig' behavior (AAA dig brushes carve along inverse-normal with a sharper falloff and terrain-floor clamp), and shares all of raise's gaps.
upgrade: Share a single directional-displacement kernel with raise, expose direction enum (UP/DOWN/NORMAL/INV_NORMAL), add min-height clamp, pressure, and dig-shape option.

aaa_equivalent: ZBrush Smooth brush, Blender sculpt Smooth (Laplacian/surface/enhance-detail)
strength: Uses real 1-ring adjacency (not a fixed-radius 4-neighbor hack), giving a proper uniform Laplacian on Z.
weakness: Only smooths Z (not full 3D position), uniform weighting rather than cotangent/area weights (causes shrinkage on irregular meshes), single Jacobi iteration per call (no iteration count), no Taubin lambda/mu shrinkage compensation, no detail-preserving smooth, no boundary handling - ZBrush/Blender offer Laplacian, Surface, Enhance-Detail, Taubin, and relax modes.
upgrade: Add iterations param, support cotangent weights, Taubin lambda/mu to prevent shrinkage, a surface-smooth mode that preserves XY and only relaxes along normal, plus relax-topology and enhance-detail variants.

aaa_equivalent: ZBrush Flatten/Planar/Trim Dynamic, UE Landscape Flatten (with eye-dropper)
strength: Computes a real brush-local average rather than a fixed global plane.
weakness: Flattens to unweighted mean Z of affected verts (not a fitted plane), ignores brush weights in the average (bias toward edge verts), no user-set target height / eye-dropper pick, no plane-from-first-contact 'trim' mode, no angle/slope constraint - AAA flatten brushes plane-fit via least-squares and offer 'flatten to target', 'ramp', 'contrast' modes.
upgrade: Compute a weighted least-squares plane from the affected verts, expose modes (average / first-hit / user-target / plane-fit), add contrast-blur parameter and ramp/slope constraint.

signature:
def compute_stamp_displacements(
    vert_positions_2d: list[tuple[float, float]],
    vert_heights: list[float],
    weights: list[tuple[int, float]],
    brush_center: tuple[float, float],
    brush_radius: float,
    heightmap: list[list[float]],
    stamp_strength: float = 1.0,
) -> dict[int, float]:
aaa_equivalent: World Machine/Gaea stamp macro, Houdini heightfield stamp, ZBrush Alpha stamp, UE Landscape Erosion/Stamp brush
strength: Performs explicit UV remap of brush footprint into heightmap grid and clamps UV, with a strength multiplier.
weakness: Nearest-neighbor sampling only (no bilinear/bicubic) causing blocky artifacts, axis-aligned square footprint inside a circular brush, no rotation / scale / flip, no blend modes (add/max/min/subtract/replace), no height remap curve, no wrap/tile, no masked alpha channel - AAA stamps support rotation, 8+ blend ops, bicubic sampling, and remap curves.
upgrade: Add bilinear/bicubic sampling, rotation/scale/flip of the stamp, blend modes (add/max/min/replace/multiply/subtract), height remap curve, tile/wrap option, and a separate mask channel; decouple stamp footprint from circular brush weight.

aaa_equivalent: UE Landscape Sculpt mode / Blender Sculpt operator / ZBrush stroke dispatch
strength: Provides a single dispatch entry-point covering 5 operations with clean bmesh lifecycle (try/finally free), validation, and a structured return payload including affected vertex count.
weakness: Not a true stroke system - a single dab per call with no stroke spacing / path / smoothing-between-dabs, full mesh read/write every call (O(N) each), no undo stack / layer, no symmetry (X/Y/radial), no multires / sculpt-mode integration, no mask/vertex-group support, no normal/position updates beyond Z, no dirty-rect optimization - AAA sculpt dispatchers stream strokes, write only touched verts, push to an undo stack, and support symmetry + mask + layers.
upgrade: Accept a stroke (list of samples + pressure), build/maintain a vertex KD-tree + dirty-vert set across dabs, add symmetry planes, mask/vertex-group weighting, undo layer stack, and an option to modify XYZ along normal; batch bmesh writes per stroke not per dab.
